definition: Clinging to views, doctrines, or fixed standpoints as possessions or identities.
notes: The project keeps clinging to views as the default because it is direct and doctrinally clear. View-clinging is acceptable in compact analytical prose. The term belongs to the fourfold upādāna framework and should not be reduced to merely having opinions. The point is appropriative fixation on views.

context_rules.context: In compressed doctrinal prose or list form
In explanatory prose emphasizing the appropriating function of upādāna
context_rules.notes: Use when the repo's broader upādāna logic should remain explicit.

example_phrases.notes: One member of the fourfold set.

version_notes: Promoted from a stub to a major entry governing the view-based member of the four clinging types.
